findings.title: Presence Indicators Dependency
findings.description: The implementation of real-time presence indicators is a prerequisite for enabling simultaneous editing. This feature must be developed before real-time co-editing can be fully realized.
assumptions: Presence indicators will be implemented using WebSocket technology.

evidence.excerpt: Requested feature: real-time presence avatars in the document header showing active viewers and editors.

recommendations: Prioritize the development of presence indicators in the MVP.

findings.title: Conflict Resolution Strategy Risk
findings.description: The choice of conflict resolution strategy (Operational Transformation vs CRDTs) poses a significant risk. Each approach has trade-offs that could impact user experience and data integrity.
assumptions: The team will choose a conflict resolution strategy that aligns with existing document models.

evidence.excerpt: As we move toward real-time co-editing, a formal conflict resolution strategy is required.

recommendations: Conduct a thorough evaluation of both strategies and prototype to assess feasibility.

findings.title: Mobile Editing Support Requirement
findings.description: Mobile editing support must be included in the initial development phase to ensure parity with desktop. This includes WebSocket support and a touch-friendly UI.
assumptions: Mobile users require a seamless editing experience comparable to desktop.

evidence.excerpt: As we build real-time collaboration, mobile must be a first-class client from day one.

recommendations: Integrate mobile editing support into the MVP to avoid future delays.

findings.title: Real-time Co-editing Dependency
findings.description: Real-time co-editing cannot be implemented until both presence indicators and a conflict resolution strategy are in place. These are foundational components for the feature.
assumptions: The development of real-time co-editing will follow the completion of prerequisite features.

evidence.excerpt: This ticket tracks the initiative to deliver real-time co-editing at feature parity or better.

recommendations: Plan for real-time co-editing to be developed in V2 after MVP features are validated.

summary: The analysis identifies critical dependencies and risks associated with implementing real-time collaboration features in CollabDocs. Key components such as presence indicators and conflict resolution strategies must be prioritized in the MVP phase, while real-time co-editing is planned for V2. The mobile editing experience is also highlighted as essential for initial development.
